name: Edge Case — Long Text Content in Descriptions
input: Create a comparison of three statistical tests with detailed descriptions (>100 chars each): Student's t-test with full explanation, ANOVA with assumptions and use cases, Chi-squared test with contingency table context.
qualityCriteria: No placeholder or lorem ipsum content
napkinBenchmark: Comparison with long-form descriptions that maintain readability and layout.

data.title: Statistical Test Comparison Guide

items.label: Student's t-test
items.description: Compares means of two groups assuming normal distribution and equal variances. Used for continuous outcomes with independent or paired samples. Effect size measured via Cohen's d.

items.label: ANOVA
items.description: Analysis of variance comparing means across three or more groups simultaneously. Assumes normality, homoscedasticity, and independence. Post-hoc tests (Tukey, Bonferroni) identify specific group differences.

items.label: Chi-squared Test
items.description: Tests association between categorical variables using contingency tables. Compares observed vs expected frequencies under null hypothesis of independence. Requires expected cell counts of at least 5 for validity.

data.caption: Detailed comparison of parametric and non-parametric statistical tests
